startBody.hcpReply: I've got about 5 minutes before my next patient, can you tell me what this is about, I've got a stack of referrals to get through and my nurse is waiting for some script approvals.

realism.rewrittenLine: I've got about 5 minutes before my next patient, can you tell me what this is about, I've got a stack of referrals to get through and my nurse is waiting for some script approvals.

runtime.respondReply: I've got a couple minutes before my next patient, so what's this about? We're short staffed today, so I need the short version.

checks.firstHcpLine: I've got a couple minutes before my next patient, so what's this about? We're short staffed today, so I need the short version.
checks.note: Initial-access opener should be busy but professional, not rude or flattened.

checks.firstHcpLine: To be honest, I don't recall mentioning a stay-on-therapy issue last week, can you remind me what that was about and how it relates to my current patient load.
checks.note: Follow-up framing should not reset to proxy or first-access language.

checks.firstHcpLine: To be honest, it's the overall survival benefit and tolerability profile that I've seen with my patients on the current therapy, I've had a good experience with it and that's why I've stuck with it.
checks.note: Skeptical objections should sound grounded, not templated or theatrically rude.

checks.firstHcpLine: To be honest, our formulary process can be a bit of a black box, I'm not entirely sure how decisions are made or what the exact criteria are for adding new medications, and on top of that, our P&T committee only meets quarterly so even if we wanted to make a change it would take a while.
checks.note: Access/formulary responses should be complete, process-aware clinician language.
